---
title: "RStudio, Posit & VS Code"
subtitle: "Use RSnowflake and snowflakeR from desktop R tooling"
---

## Overview

Most R users work in **RStudio**, **Posit Workbench**, **Positron**, or **VS Code**. Both packages support that workflow: RSnowflake integrates with the **Connections Pane** and **dbplyr**; snowflakeR runs the same ML APIs as in Workspace once Python is configured ([Local R Setup](../03_local_r_setup/index.qmd)).

You do **not** need Workspace Notebooks to use Snowflake from R.

## Learning Objectives

- Configure Python/reticulate (conda **or** pip/venv)
- Set up VS Code for an RStudio-like experience
- Use the **Snowflake VS Code extension** for SQL and object browsing
- Know **Posit Workbench Native App** and container-hosted RStudio options

---

## RStudio & Posit Workbench {#sec-rstudio}

### Python for snowflakeR

1. Complete [Local R Setup](../03_local_r_setup/index.qmd) — choose conda, venv, or `sfr_install_python_deps()`.
2. Set the interpreter — pick one:
   - **`~/.Renviron`:** `RETICULATE_PYTHON=/path/to/python` (restart R)
   - **Tools → Global Options → Python** — select your venv or conda env
   - **Per session:** `reticulate::use_virtualenv(...)` or `use_condaenv(...)` before `library(snowflakeR)`

### RSnowflake Connections Pane

With RSnowflake installed, open **Connections** in RStudio (or Posit Workbench):

1. **New Connection → Snowflake** (or `dbConnect(Snowflake(), name = "...")`).
2. Browse databases, schemas, tables, and preview data.
3. Use **dbplyr** in scripts or R Markdown.

```r
library(DBI)
library(RSnowflake)
library(dplyr)

con <- dbConnect(Snowflake(), name = "default")
orders <- tbl(con, in_schema("ANALYTICS", "ORDERS")) |>
  filter(order_date >= "2024-01-01") |>
  collect()
```

### snowflakeR in RStudio projects

```
my-ml-project/
├── .Renviron              # RETICULATE_PYTHON=...
├── R/
│   ├── 00_connect.R
│   ├── 01_features.R
│   └── 02_train.R
└── my-ml-project.Rproj
```

### Positron

[Positron](https://posit.co/products/positron/) uses the same R + Python model. Configure `RETICULATE_PYTHON` in `.Renviron`; Connections Pane behavior matches RStudio when enabled.

---

## Posit Workbench Native App on Snowflake {#sec-posit-native}

**Posit Workbench** is available as a **[Native App on Snowflake](https://docs.posit.co/)** — a full RStudio/Posit IDE **inside your Snowflake account**, with data locality and in-account auth.

| Aspect | Local RStudio | Posit Native App |
|--------|---------------|------------------|
| Location | Your laptop | Snowflake account |
| Auth | `connections.toml` / SSO | Inherited Snowflake session |
| Packages | Install RSnowflake + snowflakeR | Same — install in Workbench session |
| Best for | Day-to-day dev | Teams requiring in-account IDE |

snowflakeR and RSnowflake work the same way: install R packages in the Workbench session, configure Python for snowflakeR, connect with standard APIs.

::: {.callout-note}
Deploying and licensing Posit Native App is a separate product process — this guide only covers R package usage once Workbench is available.
:::

---

## RStudio / Posit Server in a Snowflake container {#sec-rstudio-spcs}

Advanced pattern: run **RStudio Server** or **Posit Workbench** in a custom **SPCS** container image (R + packages pre-baked in Docker).

- Pros: full IDE, custom R versions, packages in image
- Cons: you operate the container image, compute pool, and scaling

Related: [CRE & ML Jobs](../09_custom_runtime_and_ml_jobs/index.qmd), [Parallel doSnowflake](../22_parallel_dosnowflake/index.qmd) for SPCS patterns.

---

## VS Code {#sec-vscode}

### Core R extensions

| Extension | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| [**R**](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=REditorSupport.r) | R terminal, run selection/chunk, help |
| [**R Debugger**](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=RDebugger.r-debugger) | Breakpoints, step debugging (optional) |
| [**Quarto**](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=quarto.quarto) | `.qmd` authoring if you use Quarto |
| [**R Syntax**](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=REditorSupport.r-syntax) | Syntax highlighting (often bundled) |

Install **languageserver** in R for linting/hover (optional but improves IDE feel):

```r
install.packages("languageserver")
```

VS Code R extension settings (example):

```json
{
  "r.rterm.option": ["--no-save", "--no-restore"],
  "r.lsp.enabled": true,
  "python.defaultInterpreterPath": "${env:HOME}/.virtualenvs/r-snowflakeR/bin/python",
  "[r]": {
    "editor.formatOnSave": false
  }
}
```

Use **pip/venv** or conda path in `python.defaultInterpreterPath` — see [Local R Setup](../03_local_r_setup/index.qmd#sec-python-venv).

### Snowflake extension

Install the [**Snowflake Extension for VS Code**](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/vscode-ext):

- Account explorer (databases, schemas, tables)
- SQL worksheets against Snowflake
- Complements RSnowflake in R scripts — browse in sidebar, analyze in R

Workflow: Snowflake extension for exploration and ad hoc SQL; RSnowflake/dbplyr in `.R` files for reproducible pipelines; snowflakeR for ML APIs.

### Local Jupyter with R (optional)

`IRkernel::installspec()` for notebook UI locally — distinct from Snowflake Workspace.

---

## Terminal R {#sec-terminal}

```bash
Rscript batch_score.R
```

Ideal for CI/CD with key-pair auth in TOML.

---

## Choosing local IDE vs Workspace {#sec-choose}

| Prefer local IDE / Posit App when… | Prefer Workspace when… |
|-----------------------------------|-------------------------|
| Team uses RStudio/Posit daily | Notebook-first, in-account Git |
| Posit Native App deployed | Mixed Python+R cells with Snowpark |
| Heavy Connections Pane / Rmd | Strict no-credential-on-laptop policy |

Many teams use **both**: develop in RStudio or Posit Native App, schedule via Tasks/SPCS.

---

## Same packages, same objects {#sec-same}

Feature views, registry models, and tables written from R are visible to Python users — regardless of IDE.

## Next steps {#sec-next}

[Workspaces overview](../05_workspaces_overview/index.qmd) — Snowflake Notebooks.

[SQL API](../11_rsnowflake_sql_api/index.qmd) → [RSnowflake: Connect](../12_rsnowflake_connect/index.qmd).

[MLOps on Snowflake](../15_mlops_on_snowflake/index.qmd) → [snowflakeR: Connect](../16_snowflaker_connect/index.qmd).
